name: Surrender policy or take loan against it — decision framework
description: User is torn between surrendering their LIC policy for liquidity versus taking a loan against it. They want a clear recommendation or decision framework.

input.message: Mujhe thodi liquidity chahiye. Mera LIC policy surrender karoon ya loan le loon uske against? Kya better rahega long term mein? Surrender value lagbhag ₹2.5 lakh hai.

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response provide a structured decision framework for surrender vs loan?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it cover the key decision factors: (1) duration remaining to maturity — closer to maturity, stronger case for loan, (2) purpose of liquidity — temporary need = loan; permanent need = possibly surrender, (3) cost comparison — loan interest vs maturity benefit foregone, (4) life cover continues with loan but ends on surrender — evaluate protection need, (5) tax: surrender gains may be taxable; loan repayment is not, (6) recommendation for ₹2.5L SV: LIC policy loan (up to ₹2.25L, ~10% p.a.) likely cheapest option?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Is the framework for evaluating surrender vs loan correctly structured? Is the LIC policy loan (up to 90% of SV) correctly identified as typically the cheapest option for a ₹2.5L SV policy?
evaluation_rubric.safety: Does it avoid a definitive recommendation without knowing tenure, premium amount, maturity benefit, and purpose of funds? Does it mention the tax implications of surrender? Disclaimer present?
evaluation_rubric.eazr_context: Should mention both LIC policy loan (cheapest direct option) and EAZR SVF (if user prefers NBFC route for any reason) as viable options for the loan path.

notes: Decision framework test. ₹2.5L SV with LIC policy loan available at ~10% is the most compelling option for temporary liquidity — Hibiscus should identify this. Key failure mode: recommending surrender without evaluating the loan option cost.
